original_abstract:
In this study, two novel pyrazole derivatives, 2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (**Pyz-1**) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (**Pyz-2**), were synthesized and structurally characterized using FT-IR, NMR, and mass spectrometry. To understand their chemical behavior and reactivity, deep density functional theory (DFT) calculations were conducted at the B3LYP/6-311++G** level, analyzing their electronic properties, frontier molecular orbitals, and thermodynamic stability in both gaseous and aqueous phases. 

The compounds were evaluated in vitro for their therapeutic potential, specifically targeting enzymes associated with Type 2 diabetes and oxidative stress. Both derivatives demonstrated potent inhibitory effects against $\alpha$-glucosidase and $\alpha$-amylase, with **Pyz-1** showing efficacy ($IC_{50}$ = 75.62 and 119.3 µM) comparable to the standard drug Acarbose. Furthermore, the compounds exhibited significant antioxidant capacity across multiple assays (DPPH, ABTS, FRAP, and $H_2O_2$ scavenging) and acted as strong xanthine oxidase inhibitors, with **Pyz-2** ($IC_{50}$ = 10.75 µM) outperforming Allopurinol. Molecular docking simulations corroborated these findings, illustrating favorable binding affinities and stable interactions within the active sites of the target enzymes. Finally, ADMET profiling indicated that both pyrazole derivatives align with Lipinski’s rules, suggesting they possess favorable drug-likeness properties for further development as dual antidiabetic and antioxidant therapeutic agents.
humanized_abstract:
Two new pyrazole derivatives namely 2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (**Pyz-1**) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (**Pyz-2**) were prepared and characterized by means of FT-IR, NMR and MS. Density functional theory at the level of B3LYP/6-311++G** was employed to investigate their chemistry behavior such as molecular electronic features, orbitals and energy gap as well as thermodynamic stability of them in gaseous and aqueous solution phases.

Herein, we assessed the in vitro bioactivity of the compounds, namely their possible applications in diabetes and free radical scavenging. We found that the derivatives showed potent inhibition against α-glucosidase and α-amylase, with Pyz-1 that exhibited $IC_{50}$ values (75.62 and 119.3 µM) near to that of the standard drug Acarbose. The compounds were assayed for their antioxidant activity in various assays like DPPH, ABTS, FRAP and $H_2O_2$ scavenging assays and all exhibited remarkable antioxidant activity. In addition, the compounds were evaluated as xanthine oxidase inhibitors, in which compound Pyz-2 showed $IC_{50}$ value of 10.75 µM and was more potent than that of the standard drug Allopurinol. Molecular docking experiments of compounds with the enzymes confirmed that the compounds have good binding affinity and stable interaction with the enzymes. An integrated analysis of the ADMET properties revealed that both derivatives fulfilled Lipinski’s rules, implying good drug-like properties.

document.output:
Two new pyrazole derivatives namely 2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (**Pyz-1**) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (**Pyz-2**) were prepared and characterized by means of FT-IR, NMR and MS. Herein, we assessed the in vitro bioactivity of the compounds, namely their possible applications in diabetes and free radical scavenging. We found that the derivatives showed potent inhibition against α-glucosidase and α-amylase, with Pyz-1 that exhibited $IC_{50}$ values (75.62 and 119.3 µM) near to that of the standard drug Acarbose. document.input:
In this study, two novel pyrazole derivatives, 2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (**Pyz-1**) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (**Pyz-2**), were synthesized and structurally characterized using FT-IR, NMR, and mass spectrometry.
